recipe.title: Beautiful lamb shanks
recipe.description: Jamie Oliver's beautiful lamb shanks recipe makes for a killer dinner; red chillies are rehydrated in apple juice with raisins creating a sticky sweetness.

steps.descr: Preheat your oven to full whack
steps.instructions: Preheat your oven to full whack. Put the dried chillies, raisins and apple juice in a bowl, then set aside.

on_enter.say: Preheat your oven to full whack. Put the dried chillies, raisins and apple juice in a bowl, then set aside.

steps.descr: Put the lamb shanks in a roasting tray, then rub all...
steps.instructions: Put the lamb shanks in a roasting tray, then rub all over with oil, sea salt and black pepper. Bang them in the oven for 20 minutes.

on_enter.say: Put the lamb shanks in a roasting tray, then rub all over with oil, sea salt and black pepper. Bang them in the oven for 20 minutes.

steps.descr: As they’re browning, peel and finely chop the onions,...
steps.instructions: As they’re browning, peel and finely chop the onions, peel and finely slice the garlic, then halve and deseed the chillies.

on_enter.say: As they’re browning, peel and finely chop the onions, peel and finely slice the garlic, then halve and deseed the chillies.

steps.descr: Get a casserole pan that will fit the shanks quite...
steps.instructions: Get a casserole pan that will fit the shanks quite snugly — about 24cm in diameter and 10cm deep. Put it on a medium heat and add a lug of olive oil, the onions, garlic, fresh chilli, smoked paprika, bay leaves and rosemary. Leave to sweat for about 10 minutes.

on_enter.say: Get a casserole pan that will fit the shanks quite snugly — about 24cm in diameter and 10cm deep. Put it on a medium heat and add a lug of olive oil, the onions, garlic, fresh chilli, smoked paprika, bay leaves and rosemary. Leave to sweat for about 10 minutes.

steps.descr: Once the onions have softened, put the rehydrated...
steps.instructions: Once the onions have softened, put the rehydrated chillies and raisins into a food processor or liquidiser, along with any leftover apple juice, then blitz (feel free to sneak in a swig of red wine or bourbon here!) until you’ve got a paste. Stir this into your onions.

on_enter.say: Once the onions have softened, put the rehydrated chillies and raisins into a food processor or liquidiser, along with any leftover apple juice, then blitz (feel free to sneak in a swig of red wine or bourbon here!) until you’ve got a paste. Stir this into your onions.

steps.descr: Take your shanks out of the oven and turn the...
steps.instructions: Take your shanks out of the oven and turn the temperature down to 140°C/275°F/gas 1.

on_enter.say: Take your shanks out of the oven and turn the temperature down to 140°C/275°F/gas 1.

steps.descr: Add the shanks to the casserole pan along with your...
steps.instructions: Add the shanks to the casserole pan along with your tinned tomatoes and stock (if using) or equal amounts of water.

on_enter.say: Add the shanks to the casserole pan along with your tinned tomatoes and stock (if using) or equal amounts of water.

steps.descr: Stir everything together, cover and put in the oven to...
steps.instructions: Stir everything together, cover and put in the oven to stew for 2 hours 30 minutes to 3 hours, or until the meat is falling off the bone and the sauce is thick, topping up with water or stock as you go.

on_enter.say: Stir everything together, cover and put in the oven to stew for 2 hours 30 minutes to 3 hours, or until the meat is falling off the bone and the sauce is thick, topping up with water or stock as you go.

steps.descr: Take it out of the oven, have a taste and season with...
steps.instructions: Take it out of the oven, have a taste and season with salt and pepper and a dash of vinegar. Serve with steamed greens, mash, flatbreads, or whatever you fancy.

on_enter.say: Take it out of the oven, have a taste and season with salt and pepper and a dash of vinegar. Serve with steamed greens, mash, flatbreads, or whatever you fancy.
